content: re https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CAKwvOdmhRuF5eTZ2ztZBrL6BvDkA57B7OfVuvCaEMfV8nkLXCQ@mail.gmail.com/ the rustc-demangle crate supports the v0 symbol mangling scheme. It is also what the backtrace crate uses. The backtrace crate is used for the bracktraces shown when you panic.
Thanks @bjorn3! I think Linus wants to store the pre-mangled symbols anyway, so it should be easy to support (i.e. done at compilation-time).
I am a bit worried about the symbol size if they are pre-demangled
v0 symbol mangling scheme can compress repetitive substrings, which is quite common for, e.g. trait impl
Even if they are saved without mangling, they could still be compressed.
